chunks.heading: Secondary Unit Designators — Placement
chunks.text: Secondary address unit designators (such as APARTMENT or SUITE) must appear on the mailpiece for addresses with secondary units. The preferred placement is at the end of the Delivery Address Line. When the Delivery Address Line is too long to include the secondary address information, place the secondary address information on the line immediately above the Delivery Address Line.

chunks.heading: Secondary Unit Designators — Standard Abbreviations
chunks.text: Common secondary unit designators and their Postal Service standard abbreviations include: APARTMENT = APT; BUILDING = BLDG; FLOOR = FL; SUITE = STE; UNIT = UNIT; ROOM = RM; DEPARTMENT = DEPT. Use the approved abbreviation for a standardized address.

chunks.heading: Pound Sign Usage
chunks.text: If the pound sign (#) is used, there must be a space between the pound sign and the secondary number. The pound sign (#) should not be used as a secondary unit designator if the correct designation, such as APT or STE, is known or is shown in the ZIP+4 file. Note that Private Mail Box (PMB) addresses may prohibit pound sign usage.
